---
title: "About"
toc: false
---

## Who I am

I'm a clinical biostatistician with a PharmD and MPH (biostatistics/epidemiology). I work in oncology, dermatology, urology, and other therapeutic areas — primarily in SAS — and I'm transitioning toward HEOR/RWE methodology with R as my main tool.

## What I'm working toward

- **Short term:** R proficiency for clinical and HEOR work; an applied portfolio project (ZINB model comparison on MEPS utilization data).
- **Medium term:** A talk at R/Pharma; a submission to ISPOR; transition into an HEOR/RWE-focused role.
- **Long term:** Senior expertise in real-world evidence methodology.

## Why I'm publishing this

Three reasons. First, writing forces clarity — if I can't explain a concept, I don't really understand it. Second, future me will forget things, and a searchable reference saves hours. Third, the path from clinical SAS programming into HEOR/RWE isn't well-documented for people in my position, and I want what I learn to be useful to the next person.

## How to reach me

GitHub issues on the [repo](https://github.com/Priya1632/Learning-Journey.git) are the best place for corrections or suggestions. For longer conversations, LinkedIn.
